Let us proceed with the analysis of the data following the common agentic
[data analysis workflow](/methods/common-workflow.qmd).


### Step 1. Set the Overall Objective

```{.markdown file-name="Prompt"}
You are my Stata co‑programmer. The objective is to harmonize multiple survey
waves into a single dataset with consistent variable names, types, units, labels,
categories, and same recall periods.
```

### Step 2. Define inputs and outputs requirement

```{.markdown file-name="Prompt"}
The final dataset should be in format, where each row represents individual-year
observation, while each collumn represents harmonized variables. The dataset
should be analysis-ready, with clear variable names, labels, and documented
harmonization rules. The code should be modular, well-commented, and follow best
practices for reproducibility and data safeguarding. Do not write any code yet.

It should harmonize the data to the specifications describes in [Link to the Requirements].

I would like to harmonize only following modules: [demographics, income, and consumption].
```

```{.markdown file-name="Prompt"}
Input data is located on the local machine at `OneDrive/.../ai4coding-data/mex/
in subfolders 2016 and 2018. It is a read-only file. You are not
allowed to access the data directly or write code that accesses the data directly.
You must write code in the way that path to the data  is specified as
an input parameter, and the code runs without modification. Do not write any code yet.
```

### Step 3. Create a data dictionary to understand the structure and content of the data

```{.markdown file-name="Prompt"}
I would liek you to write stata code in file `01-data-dictionary.do`
that creates a data dictionary for each dataset (2016 and 2018) that
captures variable names, types, units, labels, and any other relevant metadata.
This infomraiton should also include informaiton on the recall period of the
variables, units of measurments and any other relevant information

The code should:

1. Have parameter to specify path to the folder with raw data.
2. loop over all raw data files with `.dta` and `.csv` extensions, read them,
   and extract the necessary information to build the data dictionary.
3. save data dictionary as a separate file in `data/dictionary/original-file-name.md`
4. At no circumstances the code should print data in the console or load it into the context.

Do not write any code yet. First, restate the objective, constraints, and
success criteria in your own words and report any uncertainty. Only when
confirmed, proceed to write the code.
```

### Step 4. Gather relevant metadata from the internet and add it to the data dictionary

```{.markdown file-name="Prompt"}
I would like you to fetch original metadata for the datasets from the following links:
- 2016: <https://www.inegi.org.mx/rnm/index.php/catalog/310>
- 2018: <https://www.inegi.org.mx/rnm/index.php/catalog/511>
This metadata may contain detailed information on variable definitions, units of
measurement, recall periods, and other relevant information that is not
captured in the raw data files.
Save summary of the variable-specific metadata in a separate file in
`data/dictionary/metadata-year.md`.
```

### Step 5. Verify understanding and clarify uncertainties

```{.markdown file-name="Prompt"}
Before developing data cleaning code, you must first develop a data dictionary
that captures the structure and content of the data.
```


### Step 6. Develop data cleaning code iteratively based on data dictionary

```{.markdown file-name="Prompt"}
Based on the data dictionary and metadata, develop data cleaning code.


that performs the necessary data cleaning and harmonization steps to produce


Before writing code, restate the objective, constraints, and success criteria in
your own words and report any uncertainty.

What this step does
Defines what success means and what the AI is allowed to do before any technical discussion begins.
Why it matters
LLMs optimize for completion, not correctness. Without a firm objective and constraints, the model may jump to code or infer unstated rules.
What to include

Purpose of harmonization
Target output format and quality bar
Explicit instruction not to write code yet

Example prompts
Prompt A

You are my Stata co‑programmer. The objective is to harmonize multiple survey waves into a single long-format dataset with consistent variable names, types, units, labels, and validation checks.
Do not write code yet. First restate the objective, constraints, and success criteria in your own words and report any uncertainty.

Prompt B

Before coding, summarize the harmonization goal, expected outputs, and non‑negotiable constraints.
For each item, report your certainty level (high / medium / low) and flag anything that needs clarification.
